items.question: How many parents does a normal (non-merge) commit have?
items.explanation: Only merge commits have two (or more) parents.

items.question: After `git rebase main` on your feature branch, what happens to your commits' SHAs?
items.explanation: Each replayed commit has a new parent, so each gets a new hash.

items.question: What is the golden rule of rebase?
options: never rebase commits that have been pushed to a shared branch
items.answer: never rebase commits that have been pushed to a shared branch
items.explanation: Rebase rewrites SHAs and breaks teammates' clones.

items.question: Which produces the cleanest `git log` for code review?
options: merging main into the feature branch every hour
rebasing the feature branch onto main and squashing wip commits
items.answer: rebasing the feature branch onto main and squashing wip commits
items.explanation: Linear history with coherent commits is the easiest to review.

items.question: What's the difference between `--force` and `--force-with-lease`?
options: `--with-lease` refuses to push if the remote moved since your last fetch
items.answer: `--with-lease` refuses to push if the remote moved since your last fetch
items.explanation: `--with-lease` is the safer default — it prevents accidentally overwriting others' pushes.

items.question: Which command safely undoes a *pushed* bad commit on a shared branch?
items.explanation: Revert creates a NEW commit that undoes the bad one — safe on shared history.
